communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All Defense Intelligence Reference Documents (DIRDs), technical reports, deliverables, progress reports, or final reports produced by Bigelow Aerospace Advanced Space Studies (BAASS) under DIA contract HHM402-08-C-0072 for the period October 2009 through contract termination, including any deliverables not previously released through FOIA.
Any classified companion documents, classified versions, or classified annexes to any of the 38 DIRDs produced under the AAWSAP/AATIP program, specifically including DIRD_38 (the classified deliverable that has not been publicly released as of this request).
Any program close-out reports, final program assessments, or transition documents produced at the conclusion of the BAASS contract.
Any DIA documents referencing the "East Coast team" described in the BAASS Ten Month Report (p. 188) as operating "autonomously from the Las Vegas based BAASS operation" under direct DIA program manager supervision, including any deliverables, meeting records, or correspondence from that team.
Any DIA records referencing the planned "alteration of the official programmatic status of the BAASS operation" described in the Ten Month Report (p. 188) as being "already underway" as of July 2009 — specifically, what programmatic status BAASS transitioned to after the ten-month period, and what DIA component managed that successor arrangement.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jerome McClure

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any records, access logs, search requests, routing slips, or correspondence from 2022 through 2024 documenting whether the All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) requested access to DIA records relating to the STARGATE, GRILL FLAME, CENTER LANE, SUN STREAK, or GONDOLA WISH programs.
Any DIA records from 2022 through 2024 documenting the current storage location, classification status, retention schedule, or disposition of program files from those programs not currently available in the CIA CREST database.
Any DIA records documenting the destruction, transfer, or archival of STARGATE-program files during the transition from paper to electronic records in the early 1990s, referenced in the BAASS Ten Month Report (July 30, 2009, p. 186) as a period when "thousands, perhaps millions" of DIA paper files were destroyed with electronic copies made of "only a small minority."

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jerome McClure
